item.title: Khamenei martyrdom narrative smooths path for son’s rise
item.description: Regime invokes religious history to galvanise support from Shia groups after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s assassination

item.title: The thing that everyone expected to happen has happened
item.description: Trump wants the war to be over

item.title: Chris Mason: The cost of living is catapulted centre stage yet again
item.description: Labour faces pressure to stem the Middle East's impact on energy bills, writes the BBC's political editor.

item.title: Can the G7 release enough oil to calm the market?
item.description: Analysts are sceptical that releasing strategic reserves would solve the problems in the Gulf

item.title: The handymen and drivers keeping Tehran running
item.description: Even under bombardment, workers across Iran’s capital are delivering food and fixing potholes

item.title: Israel expects Lebanon offensive to outlast Iran conflict
item.description: IDF preparing extended campaign against militant group Hizbollah, according to people familiar with discussions

item.title: Rising prices, mixed messages: Iran war is fraught with political risk for Trump
item.description: The war's price, measured in damage to the economy and in political costs to Trump, is still coming into view.

item.title: Trump says Iran war will end ‘very soon’
item.description: Oil tumbles to $90 from as high as $119 even as president gives conflicting signals on plans to end conflict

item.title: Faisal Islam: Trump comments may have eased oil price spiral, but havoc remains
item.description: It has been the most volatile day of oil trading in world history, and there is much still to play out.

item.title:
IDF: 'For the second time today: the IDF has begun a broad wave of strikes in Tehran
